title: Anti-atopic effects of Cornus officinalis ethanolic extract via attenuation of basophil degranulation and inhibition of nuclear factor-kappa B activation
author: Quah, Yixian
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The scavenging activity of the extracts against DPPH free radicals (left) was evaluated. After incubation of the samples and DPPH solution at 37℃ for 30 min, the absorbance was measured at 517 nm; ascorbic acid was used as a positive control. For the FRAP assay (middle), after the incubation of the samples and FRAP reagent at 37℃ for 30 min, the absorbance was measured at 595 nm. For the ABTS assay (right), after the ABTS solution and samples were mixed, the absorbance was measured at 734 nm. The results are expressed as μM Trolox equivalent (TE), Emax, which is the value at which the maximal effect of sample was observed, and the EC 50  value, which is the TE of the sample required to affect 50% FRAP or ABTS free radical activity (n = 3). Values indicated with different letters are significantly different at  p  < 0.05, as determined by Duncan’s multiple comparison test.
